original_hypothesis.title: recover Function Lacks Whitelist Check, Allowing Unbacked Minting
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recover function in ASDUSDC allows the owner to mint asdUSDC based on the contract's balance of any ERC20 token, without checking if the token is whitelisted.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: If the owner accidentally (or maliciously, if compromised) calls recover on a worthless airdropped token, the contract will mint asdUSDC backed by garbage. This dilutes the asdUSDC supply and breaks the 1:1 backing invariant with legitimate USDC.
preconditions: Owner calls recover() with a non-whitelisted token address

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Minting protocol tokens backed by unapproved assets

analysis_questions: Does the owner have an operational need to recover non-whitelisted tokens?
If so, should it mint asdUSDC or just transfer the tokens out?
validation_requirements: Test calling recover with a dummy token

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A worthless token is sent to the ASDUSDC contract. The owner, intending to clean up the contract, calls recover() on it. The contract mints massive amounts of asdUSDC to the owner. The owner (or an attacker who compromised the key) can now withdraw real USDC, draining legitimate users.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Dilution of asdUSDC and potential theft of underlying USDC reserves
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires burning the illegitimately minted asdUSDC

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for recover with non-whitelisted tokens

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
